codeReview.summary: The feature successfully implements typed errors in frontend services. NetworkError is thrown on fetch failures in rpc-client.ts and auth-client.ts. AuthError is thrown on authentication failures (401/403 responses, missing session, auth operation failures). Most error messages are user-friendly and suitable for display to users.

issues.description: Error messages use 'Failed to...' pattern instead of user-friendly 'Unable to... Please try again.' pattern used elsewhere. Functions createListing, getListingById, updateListing, and cancelListing have non-user-friendly fallback messages.

Functions confirmPickup, markNoShow, and submitRating use 'Failed to...' error messages instead of user-friendly 'Unable to... Please try again.' pattern. Other functions in this file were updated but these were missed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The error handling pattern in rpc-client.ts wraps fetch in try-catch and throws typed errors (NetworkError for network failures, AuthError for 401/403). This is a reusable pattern that could be documented in AGENTS.md for consistency across services.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/services/rpc-client.ts:28-47 shows the fetch wrapper pattern that throws typed errors

sharedStateObservations.observation: The user-friendly error message pattern 'Unable to [action]. Please try again.' is used inconsistently across services. Some files use 'Failed to...' which is less helpful to users. AGENTS.md could document the preferred error message format.

The subscription polling functions (subscribeToStudentClaims, subscribeToListingClaims, subscribeToActiveListings) intentionally catch and log errors without re-throwing to prevent app crashes during background polling. This pattern is commented but could be documented more explicitly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/services/claims.ts:101-105, src/services/claims.ts:128-132, src/services/listings.ts:70-73 all have comments about not crashing the app for polling errors

summary: PASS with minor issues. The feature correctly implements VAL-ERR-021 (NetworkError on fetch failures) and VAL-ERR-022 (AuthError on auth failures). VAL-ERR-025 (user-friendly messages) is mostly satisfied, but some functions in listings.ts and claims.ts still use less helpful 'Failed to...' messages. These are non-blocking because the typed error infrastructure is correct and messages don't expose internal details.
